title: The Well of Echoes
body:
The road bends through gorse and thorn and ends at a low stone wall ringing an old well. The water within is dark and perfectly still. A child's iron cup lies on the rim, dry.

As you approach, you hear — faintly, as if from far down the shaft — a voice calling a name you almost recognise.

choices.label: Call down into the well, trusting your faith to guide you.
Draw water with the cup and study what comes up.
* Lean over the rim and call out a tender greeting.
Leave the well and ride on.

title: Truly Named
body:
You lean over the stone and call into the dark. Your voice returns at once — and then again, changed. A question, not an echo: "Who asks?"

You give your name without hesitation. A long silence follows. Then the water far below brightens — not with sun, for the sky is grey — and a face looks up at you that is almost your own but older, and sadder, and kind.

"Then I will remember you," the face says, and is gone.

title: Silence Below
body:
You lean over the stone and call. Your voice comes back changed — a question, not an echo: "Who asks?"

You open your mouth and cannot find the words. After a long moment, the water stirs once and goes perfectly still. Whatever was listening has lost interest.

You ride away knowing something was offered and you were not ready to receive it.

title: The Cup
body:
The cup is lighter than iron should be. You lower it on its chain — longer than the well looks deep — and draw it up brimming. The water tastes of cold stone and, faintly, of salt, though you are a hundred leagues from any sea.

When you set the cup down you see a single silver hair inside it, curled like a question mark. You sense it is important.

choices.label: Keep the hair — it may prove useful.
Return the hair to the well.

title: Dark Water
body:
The cup is heavier than it looks. You lower it into the well and draw it up, but the chain slips from your fingers and the cup clatters back into the dark. After a long moment you hear a distant splash.

Something in the silence that follows feels deliberate. You are not welcome here.

title: An Answer
body:
You lean close and call down something warm and wordless. A long pause. Then laughter rises from the well — bright, surprised, not unkind.

"A bold one," the voice says. "Come back when you have earned a name worth calling."

The water stills. But you find you are smiling, and your heart is lighter than it was.

title: No Answer
body:
You call out with all the warmth you can muster. The well sends your words back to you unchanged. Whatever is down there, it is not charmed by flattery.

You straighten, cheeks burning, and mount your horse in silence.

title: The Road Onward
body:
You remount and ride. Within a mile the gorse gives way to tilled land and the sound of the well is gone from your ears, though not, perhaps, from your memory.

(End of encounter.)

title: The Hair Kept
body:
You coil the silver hair in a scrap of cloth and tuck it into your belt pouch. As you ride on, the wind carries what might be a woman weeping, far behind you, and then nothing.

That night, beside your fire, the hair is still in the cloth. In the morning it is gone, and there is a fine line on the palm of your hand where no line was before.

(End of encounter.)

title: The Hair Returned
body:
You let the hair fall back into the cup, and the cup back into the dark. For a long moment, nothing. Then, far below, a sound like a held breath being let out.

When you ride away you feel unaccountably lighter, as if some small debt — not yours, but one you were near enough to carry — has been paid on your behalf.

(End of encounter.)
